title: Understanding Dual-Flashlight Plot
text: X-axis: Average log2 fold change (log2FC) Positive values indicate higher abundance one group, negative values indicate lower abundance. title: Understanding Dual-Flashlight Plot
text: dual-flashlight plot easiest read terms agreement two axes: Points far zero x-axis larger fold changes. Points high y-axis stronger SSMD effect sizes. what: CytokineProfileShinyApp: Comphrensive Cytokine Profiling Analysis Shiny Application — CytokineProfileShinyApp-package
title: CytokineProfileShinyApp: Comphrensive Cytokine Profiling Analysis Shiny Application — CytokineProfileShinyApp-package
text: interactive Shiny application provides comprehensive cytokine profiling analysis quality control using biologically meaningful cutoffs raw cytokine measurements testing distributional symmetry recommend appropriate transformations. Offers exploratory data analysis summary statistics, enhanced boxplots, barplots, along univariate multivariate analytical capabilities -depth cytokine profiling Principal Component Analysis based Andrzej Maćkiewicz Waldemar Ratajczak (1993) doi:10.1016/0098-3004(93)90090-R , Sparse Partial Least Squares Discriminant Analysis based Lê Cao K-, Boitard S, Besse P (2011) doi:10.1186/1471-2105-12-253 , Random Forest based Breiman, L. (2001) doi:10.1023/:1010933404324 , Extreme Gradient Boosting based Tianqi Chen Carlos Guestrin (2016) doi:10.1145/2939672.2939785 .

what: Adjust p-values using a specified method — adjust_p
title: Adjust p-values using a specified method — adjust_p
text: thin wrapper around stats::p.adjust defaults Benjamini-Hochberg procedure.  Useful unifying multiple testing adjustments across package.

title: Adjust p-values using a specified method — adjust_p
code: adjust_p(p_values, method = "BH")

title: Adjust p-values using a specified method — adjust_p
text: p_values numeric vector raw p-values. method character string specifying p-value adjustment method.  Passed directly p.adjust.  Defaults "BH" (Benjamini-Hochberg).  See p.adjust.methods options.

title: Adjust p-values using a specified method — adjust_p
text: numeric vector adjusted p-values length p_values.

what: Apply a scale transformation to numeric columns — apply_scale
title: Apply a scale transformation to numeric columns — apply_scale
text: helper function applies chosen scaling transformation specified numeric columns data frame.  Supported built-transformations include transformation ("none"), log2, log10, z-score scaling.  custom function can also supplied perform arbitrary transformations.

title: Apply a scale transformation to numeric columns — apply_scale
code: apply_scale(   data,   columns = NULL,   scale = c("none", "log2", "log10", "zscore", "custom"),   custom_fn = NULL )

title: Apply a scale transformation to numeric columns — apply_scale
text: data data.frame matrix containing data transformed. columns character vector column names transform.  NULL (default) numeric columns transformed. scale character string specifying transformation apply.  Possible values "none", "log2", "log10", "zscore", "custom".  set "custom" function specified custom_fn applied columns. custom_fn function takes numeric vector returns transformed numeric vector.  used scale = "custom".

title: Apply a scale transformation to numeric columns — apply_scale
text: data.frame dimensions data transformed numeric columns.

what: ANOVA Analysis on Continuous Variables.  — cyt_anova
title: ANOVA Analysis on Continuous Variables.  — cyt_anova
text: function performs analysis variance (ANOVA) continuous variable every categorical predictor input data. Character columns automatically converted factors; factor columns used predictors numeric columns used continuous outcomes. valid predictor (.e., one level 10 levels), Tukey's Honest Significant Difference (HSD) test conducted adjusted p-values pairwise comparisons extracted.

title: ANOVA Analysis on Continuous Variables.  — cyt_anova
code: cyt_anova(data, format_output = FALSE, progress = NULL)

title: ANOVA Analysis on Continuous Variables.  — cyt_anova
text: data data frame matrix containing categorical continuous variables. Character columns converted factors used predictors, numeric columns used continuous outcomes. format_output Logical. TRUE, returns results tidy data frame instead list. Default FALSE. progress Optional. Shiny Progress object reporting progress updates.

title: ANOVA Analysis on Continuous Variables.  — cyt_anova
text: format_output FALSE (default), list adjusted p-values Tukey's HSD tests combination continuous outcome categorical predictor. format_output TRUE, data frame columns "Outcome", "Categorical", "Comparison", "P_adj".

what: Boxplots for Continuous Variables with Optional Grouping — cyt_bp
title: Boxplots for Continuous Variables with Optional Grouping — cyt_bp
text: function generates boxplots numeric variables data frame matrix.  supports optional grouping one categorical variables. Numeric variables can scaled using various transformations plotting.  grouping used, boxplots arranged pages specified maximum number plots per page.  Plots can saved PDF file returned list ggplot2 objects.

title: Boxplots for Continuous Variables with Optional Grouping — cyt_bp
code: cyt_bp(   data,   output_file = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   bin_size = 25,   y_lim = NULL,   scale = c("none", "log2", "log10", "zscore", "custom"),   custom_fn = NULL,   font_settings = NULL,   progress = NULL )

title: Boxplots for Continuous Variables with Optional Grouping — cyt_bp
text: data matrix data frame containing numeric categorical variables. output_file Optional string specifying name file created.  NULL (default), plots drawn current graphics device.  Ensure file extension matches desired format (e.g., ".pdf", ".png", ".tiff"). group_by Optional character vector specifying one columns use grouping.  NULL (default) grouping applied. bin_size Integer.  Maximum number boxplots per page grouping used.  Default 25. y_lim Optional numeric vector giving y-axis limits plots. Applies plots. scale Character specifying transformation numeric variables. One "none", "log2", "log10", "zscore", "custom".  "custom", supply function via custom_fn. custom_fn user-supplied function transform numeric columns scale = "custom". font_settings Optional named list font sizes supported plot text elements. progress Optional.  Shiny Progress object reporting progress updates.

title: Boxplots for Continuous Variables with Optional Grouping — cyt_bp
text: Invisibly returns list ggplot objects.  output_file provided, plots written specified file.

title: Boxplots for Continuous Variables with Optional Grouping — cyt_bp
text: Shubh Saraswat

what: Calculate correlations between a target variable and all other numeric variables. — cyt_corr
title: Calculate correlations between a target variable and all other numeric variables. — cyt_corr
text: function computes Pearson Spearman correlation coefficients specified target variable numeric variables input data frame. also calculates p-values adjusted p-values (Bonferroni Benjamini-Hochberg). Optionally, can perform group-wise correlation analysis compare correlations two groups using Fisher's z-transformation.

title: Calculate correlations between a target variable and all other numeric variables. — cyt_corr
code: cyt_corr(   data,   target,   methods = c("spearman", "pearson"),   group_var = NULL,   compare_groups = FALSE,   plot = FALSE,   font_settings = NULL,   progress = NULL )

title: Calculate correlations between a target variable and all other numeric variables. — cyt_corr
text: data data frame containing variables. target character string specifying name target variable. Must numeric column data. methods character string indicating correlation method used. Can "pearson" (default) "spearman". group_var optional character string specifying name grouping variable data. provided, group-wise correlations calculated. compare_groups Logical. TRUE group_var provided least two levels, function compare correlations first two levels group_var. plot Logical. TRUE, function generate return correlation plot. font_settings Optional named list font sizes supported plot text elements. progress Optional. Shiny Progress object reporting progress updates.

title: Calculate correlations between a target variable and all other numeric variables. — cyt_corr
text: list containing: results data frame overall correlation results, including variable names, correlation coefficients (r), p-values (p), sample size (n), method, adjusted p-values (p_bonf, p_bh), sorted absolute correlation coefficient descending order. results_groupwise data frame group-wise correlation results, group_var provided. Includes similar columns results, plus group identifier. results_diff data frame results correlation comparison first two groups, compare_groups TRUE group_var least two levels. Includes variable, r_diff (difference correlations), z (Fisher's z-score), p_diff (p-value difference), adjusted p-values (p_diff_bonf, p_diff_bh). plot correlation plot, plot TRUE.

title: Calculate correlations between a target variable and all other numeric variables. — cyt_corr
text: Shubh Saraswat

title: Dual-flashlight Plot. — cyt_dualflashplot
text: function reshapes input data computes summary statistics (mean variance) variable grouped specified grouping column. calculates SSMD (Strictly Standardized Mean Difference) log2 fold change two groups (group1 group2) categorizes effect strength. dual flash plot generated using ggplot2 x-axis represents average log2 fold change y-axis represents SSMD.

title: Dual-flashlight Plot. — cyt_dualflashplot
code: cyt_dualflashplot(   data,   group_var,   group1,   group2,   ssmd_thresh = 1,   log2fc_thresh = 1,   top_labels = 15,   output_file = NULL,   font_settings = NULL,   progress = NULL )

title: Dual-flashlight Plot. — cyt_dualflashplot
text: data data frame containing input data. group_var string specifying name grouping column data. group1 string representing name first group comparison. group2 string representing name second group comparison. ssmd_thresh numeric threshold SSMD value used determine significance (default = 1). log2fc_thresh numeric threshold log2 fold change used determine significance (default = 1). top_labels integer specifying number top variables (based absolute SSMD) label plot (default = 15). output_file Optional. file path save plot PDF (PNG extension .png). NULL (default), function returns ggplot object. font_settings Optional named list font sizes supported plot text elements. progress Optional. Shiny Progress object reporting progress updates.

title: Dual-flashlight Plot. — cyt_dualflashplot
text: output_file NULL, ggplot object representing dual-flash plot returned; otherwise, plot saved specified file function returns NULL invisibly.

title: Dual-flashlight Plot. — cyt_dualflashplot
text: Xiaohua Douglas Zhang Shubh Saraswat

title: Error-bar Plot — cyt_errbp
text: function generates error-bar plot visually compare different groups designated baseline group. displays central tendency (mean median) bar overlays error bars represent data's spread (e.g., standard deviation, MAD, standard error). plot can also include p-value effect size labels (based SSMD), presented either symbols numeric values, highlight significant differences magnitude effects. output filename provided plot saved disk; otherwise ggplot object returned drawn current graphics device.

title: Error-bar Plot — cyt_errbp
code: cyt_errbp(   data,   group_col = NULL,   p_lab = TRUE,   es_lab = TRUE,   class_symbol = FALSE,   x_lab = "",   y_lab = "",   title = "",   stat = c("mean", "median"),   error = c("se", "sd", "mad", "ci"),   scale = c("none", "log2", "log10", "zscore", "custom"),   custom_fn = NULL,   method = c("auto", "ttest", "wilcox"),   p_adjust_method = NULL,   label_size = 4,   n_col = NULL,   base_size = 11,   fill_palette = NULL,   font_settings = NULL,   output_file = NULL,   progress = NULL )

title: Error-bar Plot — cyt_errbp
text: data data frame containing least one numeric column grouping column. group_col Character string naming column defines groups. column coerced factor. p_lab Logical. TRUE (default) p-value labels displayed group comparisons. es_lab Logical. TRUE (default) effect-size labels displayed. class_symbol Logical. TRUE, p-value effect-size labels encoded using symbols (e.g., *, >>>). FALSE (default), numeric values shown instead. x_lab Character string x-axis label. empty default label generated. y_lab Character string y-axis label. empty default label generated. title Character string plot title. empty default title generated. stat Character. Central tendency statistic use. One "mean" (default) "median". error Character. Error measure visualized around statistic. One "se" (standard error; default), "sd" (standard deviation), "mad" (median absolute deviation), "ci" (approximate 95% confidence interval). scale Character controlling data transformation analysis. One "none" (default), "log2", "log10", "zscore", "custom". custom_fn user-supplied function applied numeric columns scale = "custom". method Character controlling statistical test used pairwise comparisons. One "auto" (default; choose t-test Wilcoxon based normality test), "ttest", "wilcox". p_adjust_method Character. non-NULL, specifies method passed p.adjust() correct p-values across comparisons (e.g., "BH" Benjamini-Hochberg). NULL (default) skips adjustment. label_size Numeric. Font size p-value effect-size labels. Default 4. n_col Integer. Number columns facet grid. NULL (default), uses min(3, number_of_cytokines). base_size Numeric. Base font size plot theme. Default 11. fill_palette Character vector colors group bars. NULL (default), bars filled "gray80". Supply one color per group level (e.g. c("#4E79A7", "#F28E2B",   "#E15759")). font_settings Optional named list font sizes supported plot text elements. output_file Optional file path. provided, plot saved using ggsave(); otherwise plot returned automatically printed. progress Optional. Shiny Progress object reporting progress updates.

title: Error-bar Plot — cyt_errbp
text: ggplot object. output_file specified plot saved disk returned invisibly.

title: Error-bar Plot — cyt_errbp

what: Univariate Tests for Multi-Level Categorical Predictors — cyt_univariate_multi
title: Univariate Tests for Multi-Level Categorical Predictors — cyt_univariate_multi
text: cyt_univariate_multi provides univariate statistical testing categorical predictors two levels. one-way designs, categorical predictor numeric outcome pair receives global test followed pairwise comparisons appropriate. Users may choose classical ANOVA Tukey's Honest Significant Difference (HSD) non-parametric Kruskal-Wallis test followed pairwise Wilcoxon rank-sum tests. function also supports explicit two-way ANOVA ANCOVA designs optional primary:secondary, primary:covariate, secondary:covariate interaction terms.

title: Univariate Tests for Multi-Level Categorical Predictors — cyt_univariate_multi
code: cyt_univariate_multi(   data,   method = c("anova", "kruskal"),   design = c("one_way", "two_way", "ancova"),   cat_vars = NULL,   cont_vars = NULL,   p_adjust_method = "BH",   primary_cat_var = NULL,   secondary_cat_var = NULL,   covariate_col = NULL,   include_primary_secondary_interaction = FALSE,   include_primary_covariate_interaction = FALSE,   include_secondary_covariate_interaction = FALSE,   format_output = FALSE,   progress = NULL )

title: Univariate Tests for Multi-Level Categorical Predictors — cyt_univariate_multi
text: data data frame matrix containing categorical continuous variables. Character columns converted factors. method Character specifying type global test perform. Use "anova" (default) one-way ANOVA Tukey HSD "kruskal" Kruskal-Wallis pairwise Wilcoxon tests. "kruskal" supported design = "one_way". design Character specifying model design. Use "one_way" (default) preserve existing multi-level ANOVA/Kruskal-Wallis workflow, "two_way" two-way ANOVA, "ancova" ANCOVA. cat_vars Optional character vector predictor column names used one-way workflow. NULL, factor character columns data used. cont_vars Optional character vector numeric outcome variable names. NULL, numeric columns data used, excluding covariate_col ANCOVA. p_adjust_method Character string specifying method p-value adjustment across pairwise Kruskal-Wallis follow-comparisons. Passed adjust_p. Default "BH". primary_cat_var Optional primary categorical predictor used two-way ANOVA ANCOVA workflows. secondary_cat_var Optional secondary categorical predictor used two-way ANOVA ANCOVA workflows. covariate_col Optional numeric covariate used ANCOVA workflow. include_primary_secondary_interaction Logical. Whether include primary:secondary interaction term secondary_cat_var supplied. include_primary_covariate_interaction Logical. Whether include primary:covariate interaction term covariate_col supplied. include_secondary_covariate_interaction Logical. Whether include secondary:covariate interaction term secondary_cat_var covariate_col supplied. format_output Logical. TRUE, returns list global results, pairwise results, assumption summaries; otherwise (default) returns list numeric vectors keyed "Outcome_Categorical" one-way workflow. Two-way ANOVA ANCOVA return raw model bundles keyed outcome format_output = FALSE. progress Optional. Shiny Progress object reporting progress updates analysis.

title: Univariate Tests for Multi-Level Categorical Predictors — cyt_univariate_multi
text: Either list adjusted pairwise p-values (format_output = FALSE design = "one_way") list results, pairwise, assumptions data frames (format_output = TRUE).

title: CytokineProfileShinyApp 0.0.0.9000
text: development build focuses making app easier use, easier read, reliable.

title: CytokineProfileShinyApp 0.0.0.9000
text: can now run Two-way ANOVA ANCOVA directly app. ANCOVA now supports optional secondary:covariate interactions, clearer slope checks appropriate follow-comparisons factor--covariate effects included. app now gives clearer warnings interaction effects may make results harder interpret . Many figure-based analyses now include easier text controls, can adjust titles, axis labels, legends, plot text without digging code. Random Forest XGBoost outputs now report model setup details clearly, including split settings, class balance, cross-validation status, key tuning choices.

title: CytokineProfileShinyApp 0.0.0.9000
text: Re-uploading file now refreshes saved copy right away, even file name . Excel imports reliable. previously chosen sheet longer available, app now switches safely instead failing. upload new file, saved editor choices previous file cleared cleanly. Columns look like numbers, even include symbols common missing-value markers, now likely recognized correctly. Step 2 now lets force selected columns back numeric, leftover non-numeric entries converted missing values instead blocking numeric workflows. Column selections now update smoothly, related defaults stay sync better change data choices. missing-value help now gives clearer guidance use mean, median, mode, two nearest-neighbor options, popup easier read. Two-group testing controls now label Welch t-test explicitly, manual choice matches app’s underlying behavior clearly. PLSR now handles partially missing predictor data gracefully keeping usable rows, dropping unusable predictors, giving clearer guidance sparse columns may need missing-value treatment first. uploaded data includes --range values, app now warns click Save & Use explains adjusted plain language. app now starts reliably across repo-root, installed-app, package launcher workflows. Step 2 now restores state consistently move forward return, including dynamic categorical filter selections. Fresh-start, reuse-data, upload, built-editor, Bio-Plex workflows now behave consistently recovery cleanup work. Progress notifications easier read longer analyses, cleaner spacing clearer separation main task detail text.

title: CytokineProfileShinyApp 0.0.0.9000
text: Plot text now customizable across many figure types. PCA, PLSR, sPLS-DA, MINT sPLS-DA figures now use readable default text sizes. Correlation circle plots PCA MINT sPLS-DA now behave reliably. Heatmaps now display consistently interactive use. Volcano plot now displays p-value different sizes depending small p-values . Additionally, shows upregulated vs. downregulated variables clearly.

title: CytokineProfileShinyApp 0.0.0.9000
text: result tables now labeled accurately column names contain underscores. app handles empty analysis results gracefully instead returning confusing output. XGBoost workflow now chooses best training step reliably across different scoring methods. Editing data built-editor now preserves values consistently. missing-value help popup now opens cleanly less likely cut inside app window. missing-value method selector now keeps nearest-neighbor options sync reliably instead dropping mismatching controls. PLSR now stable one component can fit, VIP>1 follow-preview now skips safely instead failing predictors remain. Deselecting categorical columns Step 2 now stays deselected instead snapping back selected. Step 2 dynamic categorical filter selections now restore correctly return Step 3. kNN (feature-wise) now safely blocks unsupported single-column use controlled message instead raw failure.

title: CytokineProfileShinyApp 0.0.0.9000
text: Several parts app cleaned reduce small startup package-loading problems. Internal helper code UI regression checks cleaned package checks behave consistently across local testing staged installs. server pipeline re-extracted dedicated mod_*_server() stage files, shared stage helpers now centralized R/app_stage_helpers.R.
